channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: El Niño is set to take hold this summer, driving up global temperatures
item.description: A potentially strong El Niño weather pattern will likely emerge this summer and persist through the rest of the year. The hottest years on record generally occur in years when El Niño is active.
item.content:encoded:
A potentially strong El Niño weather pattern will likely emerge this summer and persist through the rest of the year. The hottest years on record generally occur in years when El Niño is active.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sommer

item.title: U.S. investigates strike on Iranian school as the war sparks a global oil crisis
item.description: Trump, who promised to lower gas prices, is tapping the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as war drives prices up. And, the U.S. investigates the strike on an Iranian school that killed at least 165 people.
item.content:encoded:
Trump, who promised to lower gas prices, is tapping the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as war drives prices up. And, the U.S. investigates the strike on an Iranian school that killed at least 165 people.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: 'Songs from the Hole': The story behind JJ'88's documentary and visual album
item.description: The visual album and documentary Songs from the Hole tells the story of James Jacobs, the hip-hop artist JJ'88, as he reflects on his coming-of-age within California's state prison system.
item.content:encoded:
Songs from the Hole. Before the song starts, protagonist and producer James "JJ'88" Jacobs describes meditating on his and others' redemption while incarcerated and in solitary confinement.'/>

The visual album and documentary Songs from the Hole tells the story of James Jacobs, the hip-hop artist JJ'88, as he reflects on his coming-of-age within California's state prison system.

(Image credit: Courtesy of Netflix)
item.dc:creator: Kara Frame

item.title: Oil price surges as Iran steps up attacks on ships in the Persian Gulf
item.description: Markets seesawed on Day 13 of the war in the Middle East, as two oil tankers were struck by projectiles near Iraq's southern ports and attacks between Israel and Hezbollah intensified.
item.content:encoded:
Markets seesawed on Day 13 of the war in the Middle East, as two oil tankers were struck by projectiles near Iraq's southern ports and attacks between Israel and Hezbollah intensified.

(Image credit: Bilal Hussein)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Easy-to-use solar panels are coming, but utilities are trying to delay them
item.description: Utilities are convincing lawmakers around the U.S. to delay bills that would allow people to buy solar panels, plug them into an outlet and begin generating electricity.
item.content:encoded:
Utilities are convincing lawmakers around the U.S. to delay bills that would allow people to buy solar panels, plug them into an outlet and begin generating electricity.

(Image credit: David J. Phillip)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady

item.title: 5 ways to resist the urge to keep looking at your phone
item.description: So you want to spend less time on your phone. How do you do that when it's designed to suck you in? Life Kit spoke to experts in behavioral science, psychology and technology for real-world advice.
item.content:encoded: So you want to spend less time on your phone. How do you do that when it's designed to suck you in? Life Kit spoke to experts in behavioral science, psychology and technology for real-world advice.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Trump's war with Iran is angering some swing voters who want money spent at home
item.description: Swing voters who helped reelect President Trump in 2024 don't support his decision to go to war in Iran and instead want to see U.S. tax dollars spent tackling economic pressures facing Americans.
item.content:encoded:
Swing voters who helped reelect President Trump in 2024 don't support his decision to go to war in Iran and instead want to see U.S. tax dollars spent tackling economic pressures facing Americans.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: The Trump administration's crackdown on immigrant truckers shifts into higher gear
item.description: The White House wants tougher rules for commercial licenses after several high-profile crashes involving foreign-born drivers. But critics say that would do little to make the nation's roads safer.
item.content:encoded:
The White House wants tougher rules for commercial licenses after several high-profile crashes involving foreign-born drivers. But critics say that would do little to make the nation's roads safer.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: To stop Africa from hemorrhaging local talent, scientist creates drug discovery lab
item.description: A Zambian scientist is on a quest to prevent brain drain from Africa so he's established a state-of-the-art drug discovery lab in South Africa.
item.content:encoded: A Zambian scientist is on a quest to prevent brain drain from Africa so he's established a state-of-the-art drug discovery lab in South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Bam Adebayo's 83-point night was one to remember. But not everyone was pleased
item.description: Detractors point to Adebayo's one-of-a-kind stat line — 43 field goal attempts, 22 3-point attempts and, most of all, NBA records of 36 free throws and 43 attempts — as proof of stat-padding.
item.content:encoded:
Detractors point to Adebayo's one-of-a-kind stat line — 43 field goal attempts, 22 3-point attempts and, most of all, NBA records of 36 free throws and 43 attempts — as proof of stat-padding.

(Image credit: Rebecca Blackwell)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
